column1: # Verus verified-count manifest — the machine-readable twin of the trusted-base
# ledger's ## Baselines section (doc/guidelines/verus_trusted-base.md), and the
# Verus analogue of the TLC coverage ledger (tools/tla/model-manifest.tsv).
# tools/verus/verus-gate.sh reads it to cold-verify every gated crate and assert
# each crate's verified-obligation count; the CI verus job runs that gate, so a
# silently-dropped obligation — Verus proving *less* while still exiting 0 —
# fails a PR, not only a local run. scripts/verus-baseline.sh reads the same
# crate + flags columns for its timing sweep, so there is one source of truth for
# the gated-crate set.
# The verified count is the crate's proof-obligation count: a DROP means Verus is
# proving fewer obligations (a coverage shrink), even when the run still exits 0.
# It is asserted against a real COLD run — the gate `cargo clean -p <crate>`s
# first, so a stale target/ cannot false-green: a missing `verification results::`
# line is itself a hard failure (CLAUDE.md / doc/guidelines/verus.md, "scoped runs
# can false-green from stale cache").
# Re-derive every count from a real cold verify — never trust a stale table. `-`
# means "not yet pinned": the gate treats it as a fail-loud misconfiguration (it
# would otherwise disarm the guard), so replace `-` with the run's number to arm
# it. These counts must equal the ledger ## Baselines Result cells.
# flags are the crate's extra `cargo verus verify` arguments: --no-default-features
# for the Vec/serde-heavy feature-agnostic cores, --lib to skip a placeholder bin;
# empty for the plain crates. Rows are in CI order (the .github/workflows/ci.yml
# verus job), which is also a dependency order, so each crate's gated deps are
# already warm when it verifies.
# Columns (tab-separated): crate  flags  expected_verified
crate
kcore
ipc
